problem_statement: AI-generated code tends to fail quietly—preserving the surface appearance of function while silently violating guarantees—and standard code review and static analysis are not calibrated to detect this class of failure-untruthful behavior.
magnitude_evidence: AI-attributed files show 0.435 high-severity findings per file versus 0.242 in human controls, a 1.80× excess (955 vs 955 matched files); Study 1 found 4,120 findings across 1,643 files in a six-system enterprise environment
insufficiency_evidence: An LLM evaluator applied to the same surfaces exhibits the same suppression pattern at a 44:1 ratio below deterministic detection; for deliberation.py the LLM returned PASS on all 7 static FAIL checks (100% suppression); standard static analysis (SonarQube, Semgrep) can pass while code exhibits pervasive fail-soft behavior
solution_mechanisms: deterministic-first scanner with optional LLM augmentation
CLI and web scanner with aggregate-only research pipeline
